staged_hits.title: Operator-spectrum and entanglement-Hamiltonian route to algebraic reformulation
staged_hits.summary: For this topic, the most credible algebraic foothold inside MIPT is not factor type but the pair of diagnostics formed by operator scaling / multifractality and the entanglement-Hamiltonian response to weak measurement. These observables can anchor a later algebraic reformulation before any comparison with quantum-gravity observer algebras.

staged_hits.title: Do not equate MIPT with a von Neumann factor transition
staged_hits.summary: The current topic sources support entanglement, operator-scaling, and entanglement-Hamiltonian diagnostics for monitored dynamics, while the quantum-gravity sources discuss observer or relational observable algebras with explicit von Neumann-algebra structure. No registered source currently warrants a direct factor-type identification for MIPT phases.
